insights.insight: 3+ strategy agreement has 2x better win rate than 2-strategy agreement
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: Never risk more than 1-2% per trade. 5 losses at 2% = 10% drawdown, recoverable
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: Trending markets favor regime_trend strategy. Ranging markets favor confidence_scorer
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: Best signals come Tuesday-Thursday. Weekend markets are thin and unreliable
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: Take 40-50% profit at TP1, move stop to breakeven, let rest ride to TP2
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: Meme coins move 2-5x faster than BTC. Use wider stops and lower leverage
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: When BTC dumps, alts dump 2-3x harder. Never long alts into a BTC nuke
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: The best trade is often no trade. Cash has zero drawdown. Patience > action
insights.evidence: Student course foundational knowledge

insights.insight: HYPE_BUY is the highest-edge setup. 30-day backtest: 58% WR, PF 1.61 (36 trades). During 18-06 UTC: 62% WR, PF 2.19. The sniper filter correctly identifies this as A+ grade.
insights.evidence: 30-day 1h OHLCV backtest, 500 candles, 8 parameter configs tested. Source: deep_edge_analysis.py, full_stack_backtest.py

insights.insight: HYPE_BUY performs best during 18-06 UTC (evening/night sessions). WR=67% PF=2.47 vs daytime 06-18 UTC WR=47% PF=1.29. US evening and Asian morning are the high-edge windows.
insights.evidence: 30-day time-of-day analysis. 18 trades in 18-24 UTC window, 19 trades in 06-12 UTC. Source: deep_edge_analysis.py

insights.insight: SOL_SELL is marginal. 30-day backtest: PF < 1.0 across all 8 parameter configs. Best case PF 0.97. Keep for data collection but size conservatively (0.5x normal). LLMs should learn when SOL_SELL works vs fails.
insights.evidence: 30-day parameter sweep, 8 configs x 36 trades each. Source: deep_edge_analysis.py

insights.insight: MFE/MAE analysis shows current TP (3.75%) is near-optimal. Median MFE=3.6%, 75th percentile=6.5%. Wider TP (6%) captures fat tails but lowers WR. Current SL (2.5%) survives most noise (median MAE=2.1%).
insights.evidence: MFE/MAE distribution on 35 HYPE_BUY trades over 30 days. Source: deep_edge_analysis.py

insights.insight: In choppy markets, only 1 strategy fires per symbol per scan. Solo signals routed to sniper via callback still have edge. The strategy pipeline (especially multi_tier_quality) adds real alpha: blind entry WR=56% vs strategy-filtered WR=58-94% depending on sample.
insights.evidence: Live signal scan + replay analysis. Signal flow fix commit 9373a3d. Source: signal_scan.py, clean_replay.py

insights.insight: HYPE_BUY losers hit SL within 1-2 bars. Winners take 6-12 bars. If a HYPE_BUY trade survives 5+ bars, it is very likely a slow winner — HOLD and extend time stop. Do NOT cut early.
insights.evidence: Bar-by-bar resolution analysis on 49 replay trades. Source: clean_replay.py

insights.insight: RSI 35-65 is the sweet spot for HYPE_BUY entries (62-64% WR). Oversold (RSI<35) shows only 50% WR — counterintuitive but validated. RSI 55-65 has highest WR at 64%.
insights.evidence: RSI zone analysis on 84 HYPE_BUY entries over 30 days. Source: deep_edge_analysis.py

insights.insight: BTC_BUY shows 56% WR, PF 1.40 over 30 days but is not yet in the sniper filter proven setups. Monitor in sim. BTC_SELL and HYPE_SELL are confirmed negative EV — hard blocked.
insights.evidence: 30-day parameter sweep. BTC_BUY: 36 trades. HYPE_SELL: 0% WR. Source: deep_edge_analysis.py

insights.insight: BTC >0.5% hourly move predicts HYPE direction next hour with 73% accuracy (63 samples). BTC >1% move = 79% (15 samples). Avg follow: +0.62% up, -0.75% down. Tradeable lead-lag signal.
insights.evidence: 30-day 1h correlation on 500 aligned candles

insights.insight: BTC lead-lag ONLY works for swing entries (2.5%+ TP, 6x lev, 12h hold). Tight scalps (0.5-1.5% TP) FAIL with BTC trigger — follow-through takes 6-12 bars. BTC >0.8% trigger + HYPE swing = 77% WR PF 3.72 (13 trades). Do NOT scalp on BTC signals.
insights.evidence: Multi-config backtest: 5 configs x 4 BTC thresholds. Scalps PF<1 across all. Swings PF 3+ at >0.8% threshold.

insights.insight: Pure scalps (0.3-0.8% SL) on HYPE have 40-46% WR without strong directional trigger — barely profitable via R:R. The edge is in ENTRY TIMING (BTC trigger, regime, time-of-day), not in tight stops. Wider stops with good entries beat tight stops with any entry.
insights.evidence: Multi-edge backtest: 13 configs across 3 symbols. Tight scalps profitable only via volume (159 trades) not WR.

insights.insight: Exit strategy comparison on same entries: Fixed SL+TP beats trailing stops, partial closes, and scale-out. Tight SL (1.5%) + wide TP (5%) = best return (+5.5%). Trailing stops hit by noise before TP. Partial closes reduce upside. Keep it simple: fixed SL, fixed TP, let it resolve.
insights.evidence: 28-trade comparison, 5 exit strategies on identical entries. Source: entry utilization analysis.

insights.insight: Simple technical filters (SMA+RSI+BTC) produce 21-29% WR. Strategy pipeline produces 53-58% WR. The bot strategies ARE the edge. Simple conditions cannot replicate what multi_tier_quality + ensemble capture. Trust the pipeline, focus on utilizing entries.
insights.evidence: Confluence analysis: 37 trades at 27% WR with basic filters vs 36-39 trades at 53-58% from strategies.

insights.insight: Signal flow: 49% of hours have 1 strategy firing, 17% have 2+, 34% have 0. Solo-signal callback captures the 49% that ensemble rejects. The sniper setup filter (HYPE_BUY) is the real quality gate, not consensus count. Solo signals from proven setups are tradeable.
insights.evidence: 100-candle walkthrough of 4 strategies on HYPE 1h data.

insights.insight: 12h time stop is optimal at +4.5R net. 3h too early (-0R), 24h diminishing returns (+2.4R). Most winners resolve in 6-12 bars. Time stops at 12h cut only the trades that would have been marginal anyway. The current sim setting (24h, was 12h) could be tightened back to 12h for better risk-adjusted returns.
insights.evidence: Time stop sweep: 8 settings (3h-48h) on 28 entries, net R analysis. 12h = +4.5R, 24h = +2.4R.

insights.insight: Multi-timeframe alignment is critical: 1h+6h aligned = 33% WR, misaligned = 10% WR on HYPE BUY. The multi_tier_quality strategy already gates on 6h EMA alignment — this validates that gate. Never override 6h misalignment.
insights.evidence: 31 trades split by 1h/6h alignment. Aligned 7/21 (33%), misaligned 1/10 (10%).

insights.insight: Do NOT stop trading after losses. After 2 consecutive losses, next trade WR = 75% (n=4). Drawdowns of 5-10% recover in 1.5-4 days. The system has positive EV even at 47% WR because R:R is 1.5:1. Pausing after losses would MISS the recovery trades.
insights.evidence: 29-trade sim: 9W/10L, 00->21. 3 drawdown recovery events (1.5d, 1.6d, 3.9d avg).

insights.insight: HYPE min SL should be 1.93% (1.5x median 1h range). Current 2.5% is above noise. HYPE after >1% down bars bounces 61% of time (mean reversion edge). Volume support at 37.50 (5% below). US session (14-21 UTC) has highest volume and ranges.
insights.evidence: 500 1h candles: range stats, volume profile, momentum autocorrelation. Source: volume_profile.py

insights.insight: BTC min SL is 0.96% (1.5x median range). BTC is tighter than HYPE — can use tighter stops. Volume support at 70117 (1% below). BTC autocorrelation -0.04 (random walk at 1h). All assets show random walk — edge is in ENTRY QUALITY not trend riding.
insights.evidence: 500 1h candles per symbol. Autocorrelation, range stats, volume profile.

insights.insight: Strategy weights show vmc_cipher at 0.02 (0% WR on 42 trials) — effectively dead. regime_trend at 0.32 (25% recent WR) — struggling. confidence_scorer at 0.36 (45% recent WR) — decent. mean_reversion at 0.47 (40% recent WR, currently firing on oversold HYPE). multi_tier_quality and monte_carlo_zones at 0.50 (default, no live data yet). Consider disabling vmc_cipher to reduce noise in ensemble voting.
insights.evidence: Strategy weights from ml_data/strategy_weights.json with recent outcomes.

insights.insight: After 3+ consecutive red 1h candles on HYPE, next 6h has 79% probability of being up with +1.17% avg gain (n=48). Mean reversion is real and tradeable. After RSI enters 30-35 zone, 3h forward return is 64% up with +0.43% avg (n=28). Entry at RSI<30 is too early â€” wait for RSI recovery to 30-35.
insights.evidence: 500 1h candles, 49 events of 3+ red streaks, 29 events of RSI 30-35. Forward return analysis.

insights.insight: Solo signals (1-agree) with win_prob < 48% should be blocked. Live losses on 2026-03-25: trades 2&3 had 42% and 40% win prob, both lost. Win_prob < 50% = negative EV after fees. Added win_prob floor gate at 0.48 to sniper filter.
insights.evidence: 3 live trades on 2026-03-25: all losses. 2 were solo signals with sub-50% win prob. RSI gate added at <30, >75.

insights.insight: RSI zone analysis (500 1h candles): RSI<30 has only 10.3% WR on raw candles (terrible). RSI 30-35 has 12.5% WR but PF 3.03 (rare big winners). RSI 35-50 is best (27.8% WR, PF 1.95). RSI 65-75 surprisingly strong (29.6% WR, PF 2.73). RSI>75 decent WR but thin edge.
insights.evidence: HYPE 1h 500-candle RSI zone analysis with 2.5% SL / 3.75% TP simulation.

insights.insight: TP/Hold optimization grid (500 1h candles, SMA20 filter): 12h hold with 2.5% TP is the optimal balanced config (+37R total, 56% WR). Current config (3.75% TP, 24h hold) yields +32R at 45% WR. 24h hold only wins at 5% TP (+53R) but WR drops to 44%. 3h hold is suboptimal at ALL TP targets. For scalp-focused trading, consider 2.5% TP with 12h hold. For max R, keep 5% TP with 24h hold.
insights.evidence: 7-TP x 4-hold grid optimization on 500 1h HYPE candles with 2.5% SL and SMA20 filter.

insights.insight: CRITICAL: MIN_SIGNAL_EV in .env is 0.03 (default 0.08). At 0.03 the EV gate blocks NOTHING â€” trades with actual negative EV (42% WR, EV=-0.13) pass through. This is the #1 reason bad trades execute. Raise to 0.08 minimum. Also: ensemble win_prob deflation uses regime-dependent factors (e.g., 2-agree consolidation = 0.65x deflation). A 70% confidence becomes 45.5% win prob after deflation. Add a win_prob >= 0.48 gate to the signal pipeline to catch these.
insights.evidence: Analysis of ensemble.py lines 1689-1748, signal_pipeline.py gates 1c/1d, and 3 live losing trades. EV at 42% WR with 1.2 R:R and 1% fee drag = -0.129.

insights.insight: COMBO SIGNAL: BTC RSI<20 AND HYPE alpha >0.5% = 100% WR at 3-6h (3 events, avg +4.04% at 6h, MFE 5.91%). Wider sample BTC RSI<30 + alpha>1% = 100% WR at 6h (6/6), avg +1.92%. Edge DIES by 24h. This is a 3-12h scalp/medium-hold, NOT a trend trade. Optimal params: 2.5% SL, 3.75% TP, 12h time stop.
insights.evidence: 500 1h candle backtest, 3 exact matches + 7 wider matches. Forward return analysis at 1/3/6/12/24h horizons.

insights.insight: SOL RSI<10 is a DEATH TRAP â€” 0% up-rate at 6h (n=6), avg -4.73% at 24h. SOL RSI<20 = -1.52% at 12h with 7% WR. MFE only +0.59% vs MAE -1.94%. SOL extreme oversold is a CONTINUATION signal, not reversal. NEVER buy SOL at RSI<20.
insights.evidence: 500 1h candle cross-asset analysis. 6 events RSI<10, 28 events RSI<20.

insights.insight: HYPE decoupling signal: when HYPE alpha >1% while BTC drops >1% (n=21), HYPE is 85% up at 6h (+1.33% avg). After BTC panic (4h drop >2%, n=14), HYPE averages +2.07% at 6h. BTC panic is a HYPE-specific long trigger. Also: BTC RSI<15 predicts continued decline, NOT bounce.
insights.evidence: 500 1h candle cross-asset study, 21 decoupling events, 14 BTC panic events.

insights.insight: CRITICAL BUG FOUND: multi_tier_quality had 0.50 weight (MAXIMUM) via Laplace smoothing on zero trials, despite causing ALL 3 live losses. Zero-trial strategies got more voting power than proven performers. Fixed: zero-trial default 0.50â†’0.30, multi_tier seeded with 0/3 record (weight 0.20).
insights.evidence: Strategy weights analysis. multi_tier_quality was primary_driver on all 3 live losing trades totaling -$19.16.

insights.insight: HYPE_BUY edge trend: WEAKENING (-24.0pp). Overall: 418 trades, WR=51.7%, PF=1.34. First third WR=64.0%, last third WR=40.0%.
insights.evidence: 500 1h candle edge decay analysis, 418 trades split into thirds.

insights.insight: HYPE BUY entry timing: best=open_next (PF=1.38, WR=51.3%), worst=vwap_proxy (PF=1.25). Entry price method matters 0.13 PF difference.
insights.evidence: 5 entry methods compared on 417 HYPE BUY trades.

insights.insight: HYPE volatility regime edge: best=High Vol (P50-P75) (PF=3.51, WR=73.9%), worst=Extreme (>P90) (PF=0.65). Trade when vol is High Vol (P50-P75).
insights.evidence: 500 1h candles bucketed by ATR percentile.

insights.insight: SOL volatility regime edge: best=Normal (P25-P50) (PF=1.75, WR=61.5%), worst=Extreme (>P90) (PF=0.39). Trade when vol is Normal (P25-P50).
insights.evidence: 500 1h candles bucketed by ATR percentile.

insights.insight: BTC volatility regime edge: best=Very High (P75-P90) (PF=3.13, WR=66.2%), worst=Normal (P25-P50) (PF=0.59). Trade when vol is Very High (P75-P90).
insights.evidence: 500 1h candles bucketed by ATR percentile.

insights.insight: HYPE BUY edge by BTC correlation: best regime=Medium (0.5-0.7) (PF=2.05, WR=59.5%, 158 trades). Correlation regime affects profitability.
insights.evidence: Rolling 24h BTC-HYPE correlation bucketed, HYPE BUY backtested per bucket.

insights.insight: Signal clustering: MARGINAL - near random. After WIN: 55.2% WR. After LOSS: 46.2% WR. Autocorrelation(1)=0.090. Keep position sizing constant.
insights.evidence: Sequential trade analysis on HYPE BUY, win/loss streaks.

insights.insight: Fee impact: at 5x leverage, fees add +1.1pp to breakeven WR (BE=41.1%). At 10x, +1.1pp (BE=41.1%). Use limit orders (0.02% maker) to halve fee drag.
insights.evidence: Mathematical fee analysis at Hyperliquid rates (0.02% maker, 0.05% taker).

insights.insight: Position sizing is 72% of all losses. 4 trades at 20-37x equity = -$330 of -$470 total. Hard notional cap at 15x equity prevents catastrophic sizing. At 5x cap, same 35 trades go from -$52 to +$62.
insights.evidence: Complete system audit: 35 live trades analyzed, 4 catastrophic outliers identified. Arithmetic proof on actual PnL.

insights.insight: Trailing stop generates ALL positive alpha: +$325 from 5 trades. Everything else combined: -$399. Current trail captures only 65% of MFE (one trade captured only 19%). Wider trail (2.0%) with higher activation = more capture. But 82% of trailing profit came from 2 outlier trades -- magnitude is overfit, direction is right.
insights.evidence: 5 trailing wins vs all other trade types. MFE analysis on each. 2 outliers dominate.

insights.insight: Pipeline has 79 components: 47 gates + 32 multipliers. 91% of signals die before becoming trades. Biggest killer: duplicate position (63.6% of rejections -- structural, not analytical). Chop detector kills 48% of remaining signals. 72% of killed signals leave NO audit trail. Many gates are redundant (e.g., 4 duplicate checks, 3 correlation guards, 3 R:R checks).
insights.evidence: Complete gate audit: every gate, filter, and multiplier cataloged with file:line references. 18 REDUNDANT_WITH pairs identified.

insights.insight: Feedback systems are in deadlock: confidence calibrator frozen at -15 offset, adaptive risk stuck at 0.60x floor, Kelly at minimum. Combined multiplicative effect: positions are 1.1% of full Kelly. The system learned to be over-cautious from early oversized losses, but those were fixed by hard cap -- feedback has not reset.
insights.evidence: Feedback state inspection: cal offset -15, adaptive 0.60, Kelly floor. Product = ~0.011 of intended size.

insights.insight: CORRECTED: Mean-reversion dominates is WRONG. Hurst exponent = 0.64-0.70 for all assets = TRENDING. But trends are 12-24h scale, not multi-day. Mean-reversion works at daily scale. Match strategy to timescale: trend-follow at 12-24h, mean-revert at daily+.
insights.evidence: Hurst exponent calculation on 500 1h candles for BTC/ETH/SOL/HYPE. All show H>0.5 (trending).

insights.insight: CORRECTED: Shorts are the only edge is WRONG for 30d window. Longs outperformed on SOL (3/5 weeks) and HYPE (3/5 weeks). Shorts-only was recency bias from last 5-day selloff. Both directions have edge when entry quality is high.
insights.evidence: 5-week rolling window analysis. Long vs short PnL by symbol by week.

insights.insight: CORRECTED: Volume fading = winners is SPURIOUS. When controlling for symbol, RISING volume predicts winners on BTC/ETH/SOL. The original finding was a confound from symbol mix.
insights.evidence: Volume analysis controlled by symbol. Fading volume correlated with HYPE-specific moves, not general pattern.

insights.insight: Three strategies are dead weight and should be disabled or heavily down-weighted: vmc_cipher (5% WR on 20 trades), sniper_premium (0% WR on 20 trades), regime_trend (20% WR, spams 182 signals/day). Only confidence_scorer (50% WR, Kelly 0.28), probability_engine (45% WR), and bollinger_squeeze (40% WR, Kelly 0.18) show real signal.
insights.evidence: 20+ trade sample per strategy from live and paper trading. Kelly fractions computed from actual outcomes.

insights.insight: 40% of worst losses had the RIGHT direction. 2 of 5 worst losses had MFE of +2.5-2.9% but got stopped at -4.1% spike. Both were same SOL move on Apr 1. Lost $130, would have made $540 with wider stops ($670 swing). Tight stops at high leverage = the real killer.
insights.evidence: MFE/MAE analysis on 5 worst losses. 2 had MFE > |SL|. Both SOL SHORT on Apr 1.

insights.insight: No cross-asset lead-lag at 5min -- BTC and alts move in same bar. But fading BTC pumps on SOL = 76% WR (n=67, p=0.067). Asymmetry: fading pumps works, fading dumps does not. Order book imbalance is NOT predictive (50% accuracy = coin flip). ETH is anti-predictive (25%) suggesting spoofing.
insights.evidence: 5-min cross-asset correlation study + order book imbalance analysis. 67 BTC-pump fade events.

insights.insight: Time-of-day multipliers were INVERTED. H17 UTC (best short hour, 58-68% WR) was blocked at 0.15x. H20 (worst hour) was boosted to 1.3x. Kill zones: H14-17 UTC (US session), H00-02 (Asia). Monday is best day. Weekends have lower vol and mean-revert. Now fixed to data-driven values.
insights.evidence: 7-day 5-min analysis of win rates by hour. Every hour mapped to actual outcomes.

insights.insight: Funding rate strategy was DEAD CODE the entire time. Thresholds assumed 8h payments but Hyperliquid pays hourly. Strategy never fired for any major asset. Fix: divide thresholds by 8. Timer should check 24x/day not 3x/day. Free edge left on table.
insights.evidence: Code audit: funding thresholds 8-10x too high for Hyperliquid hourly funding. Zero fires in production.

insights.insight: Realistic targets for $500 account: 3-8% monthly return, Sharpe 1.0-1.5, PF 1.5-2.0, WR 55-65% trend / 65-80% MR. Max 2-3 trades/day to manage fee drag. Goal: compound to $2-5k where strategies become more capital-efficient. Trade less, trade bigger with Kelly sizing.
insights.evidence: Academic/industry research benchmarks. Monte Carlo simulations on $500 starting equity.

insights.insight: Counter-thesis: Bearish momentum fading, RSI divergence forming on 4H
insights.evidence: Critic predicted opposite: Momentum exhaustion risk outweighs continuation thesis
